name: Sensational Chocolate-Almond Sandwiches
recipeIngredient: 6 oz Fine-quality white chocolate, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Grind almonds with sugar in a food processor to a fine powder.
2. Sift flour, cocoa, and baking powder into a medium bowl.
3. Cream butter in a large bowl.
4. Add almond mixture; beat until smooth and fluffy.
5. Using a wooden spoon, stir in cocoa mixture. Dough will seem dry at first, but stir until it comes together.
6. Divide dough into two halves.
7. Scoop each half onto a sheet of waxed paper. Roll into logs about 1.5 inches in diameter. Wrap in waxed paper and refrigerate for 2 hours.
8. Roll each log again to an even round shape. Refrigerate for 4 hours or until firm.
9. Preheat oven rack to center position, and preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
10. Cut each log into about 1/4 inch thick slices with a thin, sharp knife. Place slices on ungreased baking sheets, about 1.5 inches apart.
11. Bake for 10-11 minutes or until cookies are just set; they burn easily on the bottom. Carefully transfer to racks using a metal spatula and cool completely.
12. For ganache: Put white chocolate in a small bowl. Bring cream to a full boil in a small, heavy saucepan. Pour over chocolate all at once. Stir with a whisk until the mixture is smooth. Cool to room temperature. Refrigerate for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally with a whisk until cold and thick but not set.
13. Whip the ganache at high speed for 3 minutes or until lighter in color and thickened.
14. Spread about 2 teaspoons of ganache on flat sides of half the cookies. Sandwich each with a flat side of a second cookie, choosing cookies of the same size to put together. Refrigerate for 15 minutes to set before serving.
15. Cookies can be kept, covered, in the refrigerator for up to 1 week and served at room temperature. Makes 28 cookie sandwiches.
